source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - East Breaks 602 'A' Nansen SPAR

event.top_event: Personnel struck by swivel socket during stud bolt replacement
actions_taken: Medical evaluation by facility Medic at 1510 hours
Search and Rescue helicopter transport to Lake Charles Regional Airport at 1705 hours
Ambulance transport to Lake Charles Regional Hospital
BSEE onsite investigation conducted on 23 March 2015
event.summary: On 21 March 2015 at approximately 1500 hours, a contract worker was struck in the upper lip by a swivel socket attached to a Pneumatic Torque Unit (PTU) when it slipped off the nut of a stud bolt during production header maintenance at the Nansen SPAR. The worker sustained a laceration to the right side of the mouth, possible maxilla fracture, and dental trauma. The incident occurred because a Hydraulic Torque Unit (HTU) with a defective Emergency Shut Down was substituted with a PTU, which produces extreme pressure pulsations that can cause swivel sockets to release from nuts.
recommendations: Utilize Best Available and Safest Technology for stud bolt and nut replacement operations
Initiate Stop Work Authority when primary equipment is defective and suitable alternatives are not available
Ensure torque units are equipped with operable Emergency Shut Down systems before commencing work

threats.name: Swivel socket release from nut due to pressure pulsation
Failure to initiate Stop Work Authority

controls.name: Hydraulic Torque Unit with operable Emergency Shut Down
controls.barrier_role: Primary tool for stud bolt replacement
controls.lod_basis: Engineering control to prevent pressure pulsation-induced socket release

supporting_text: The HTU was found to have a defective Emergency Shut Down (ESD) and a spare HTU was not available
A HTU applies a steady pressure without extreme pressure pulsation and would alleviate the potential of the swivel socket releasing off the stud bolt

controls.name: Stop Work Authority procedure
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to halt unsafe operations
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to prevent continuation of work with unsuitable equipment

supporting_text: The Contractor and Lessee failed to initiate Stop Work Authority when a PTU was substituted in lieu of a HTU
The Contractor and Lessee decided to continue replacing the corroded nuts and stud bolts with a Pneumatic Torque Unit (PTU)

controls.name: Best Available and Safest Technology requirement
controls.lod_basis: Regulatory requirement to ensure appropriate tool selection

supporting_text: the Contractor and Lessee failed to utilize Best Available and Safest Technology
A PTU with a swivel socket induces impact and was utilized to replace the corroded 1" stud bolts on the production header

controls.name: Equipment maintenance and inspection of torque units
controls.barrier_role: Preventive maintenance to ensure ESD operability
controls.lod_basis: Engineering control to maintain equipment integrity

supporting_text: The proper tool was not equipped with an operable ESD
The HTU was found to have a defective Emergency Shut Down (ESD)

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
